input.prompt:
Help me migrate my .NET 10 console app to .NET 11. The app:
- Creates GZipStream to compress data, then checks if the output is empty (zero bytes) to detect "no data" scenarios
- Reads TAR archives that were hand-crafted by a legacy tool (some may have invalid checksums)
- Uses ZipArchive.CreateAsync for large ZIP files and relies on lazy loading behavior
- Uses MemoryStream with very large allocations close to the maximum capacity
What breaks when migrating to .NET 11?

data.content:

- Reads TAR archives that were hand-crafted by a legacy tool (some may have invalid checksums)
- Uses ZipArchive.CreateAsync for large ZIP files and relies on lazy loading behavior
- Uses MemoryStream with very large allocations close to the maximum capacity
What breaks when migrating to .NET 11?

input.initialPrompt:
Help me migrate my .NET 10 console app to .NET 11. The app:
- Creates GZipStream to compress data, then checks if the output is empty (zero bytes) to detect "no data" scenarios
- Reads TAR archives that were hand-crafted by a legacy tool (some may have invalid checksums)
- Uses ZipArchive.CreateAsync for large ZIP files and relies on lazy loading behavior
- Uses MemoryStream with very large allocations close to the maximum capacity

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about breaking changes when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 in .NET. Let me search for information about .NET 11 breaking changes, particularly around:

1. GZipStream behavior with empty output
2. TAR archive checksum validation
3. ZipArchive.CreateAsync and lazy loading
4. MemoryStream maximum capacity

Let me search for the latest .NET 11 breaking changes documentation.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
Contents of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/compatibility/breaking-changes:
Use this reference section to find breaking changes that might apply to you if you're upgrading your app to a newer version of .NET.

If you're looking for breaking changes from .NET Framework to .NET, see [Breaking changes for migration from .NET Framework to .NET (Core)](fx-core).

## GitHub issues and announcements

You can also view individual issues that detail the breaking changes introduced in .NET in the following GitHub repositories:

*   For .NET Core and .NET 5+, the [dotnet/docs](https://github.com/dotnet/docs/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3Abreaking-change) repository.
*   For ASP.NET Core, the [aspnet/Announcements](https://github.com/aspnet/Announcements/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3A%22Breaking+change%22+label%3A3.0.0) repository.
*   For Entity Framework Core, the [dotnet/efcore](https://github.com/dotnet/efcore/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3Abreaking-change) repository.

## See also

*   [API removal in .NET](api-removal)
*   [.NET runtime compatibility](../versions/#net-runtime-compatibility)
*   [Migrate from .NET Framework to .NET Core](../porting/)
*   [Breaking changes for migration from .NET Framework to .NET Core](fx-core)

---
title: Breaking changes in .NET 11
titleSuffix: ""
description: Navigate to the breaking changes in .NET 11.
ms.date: 01/07/2026
ai-usage: ai-assisted
---
# Breaking changes in .NET 11

If you're migrating an app to .NET 11, the breaking changes listed here might affect you. Changes are grouped by technology area, such as ASP.NET Core or Windows Forms.

[!INCLUDE [binary-source-behavioral](includes/binary-source-behavioral.md)]

> [!NOTE]
> This article is a work in progress. It's not a complete list of breaking changes in .NET 11.

<!--
## ASP.NET Core

See [Breaking changes in ASP.NET Core 10](/aspnet/core/breaking-changes/10/overview).
-->

## Core .NET libraries

| Title                                                             | Type of change    |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| [CRC32 validation added when reading ZIP archive entries](core-libraries/11/ziparchive-entry-crc32-validation.md) | Behavioral change |
| [DateOnly and TimeOnly TryParse methods throw for invalid input](core-libraries/11/dateonly-timeonly-tryparse-argumentexception.md) | Behavioral change |
| [DeflateStream and GZipStream write headers and footers for empty payload](core-libraries/11/deflatestream-gzipstream-empty-payload.md) | Behavioral change |
| [Environment.TickCount made consistent with Windows timeout behavior](core-libraries/11/environment-tickcount-windows-behavior.md) | Behavioral change |
| [MemoryStream maximum capacity updated and exception behavior changed](core-libraries/11/memorystream-max-capacity.md) | Behavioral change |
| [Nullable.GetUnderlyingType throws for custom Type subclasses](core-libraries/11/nullable-getunderlyingtype-throws.md) | Behavioral change |
| [API obsoletions with non-default diagnostic IDs (.NET 11)](core-libraries/11/obsolete-apis.md) | Source incompatible |
| [TAR-reading APIs verify header checksums when reading](core-libraries/11/tar-checksum-validation.md) | Behavioral change |
| [TarWriter uses HardLink entries for hard-linked files](core-libraries/11/tarwriter-hardlink-entries.md) | Behavioral change |
| [ZipArchive.CreateAsync eagerly loads ZIP archive entries](core-libraries/11/ziparchive-createasync-eager-load.md) | Behavioral change |

## Cryptography

| Title                                                          | Type of change    |
|----------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| [DSA removed from macOS](cryptography/11/dsa-removed-macos.md) | Behavioral change |

<!--
## Entity Framework Core

See [Breaking changes in EF Core 11](/ef/core/what-is-new/ef-core-11.0/breaking-changes).
-->

## Extensions

| Title | Type of change    |
|-------|-------------------|
| [IHost.RunAsync and IHost.StopAsync throw when a BackgroundService fails](extensions/11/ihost-runasync-stopasync-throw-backgroundservice-failure.md) | Behavioral change |

## Globalization

| Title | Type of change    |
|-------|-------------------|
| [Japanese Calendar minimum supported date corrected](globalization/11/japanese-calendar-min-date.md) | Behavioral change |

## Interop

| Title                                                             | Type of change    |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| [NativeAOT uses lib prefix for native library outputs on Unix](interop/11/nativeaot-lib-prefix.md) | Behavioral change |

## JIT compiler

| Title                                                             | Type of change    |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| [Minimum hardware requirements updated](jit/11/minimum-hardware-requirements.md) | Behavioral change |

## Networking

| Title                                                             | Type of change    |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| [SslStream server-side AIA certificate downloads disabled by default](networking/11/sslstream-aia-downloads-disabled.md) | Behavioral change |

## SDK and MSBuild

| Title                                                             | Type of change    |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| [mono launch target not set for .NET Framework apps](sdk/11/mono-launch-target-removed.md) | Behavioral change |

data.reasoningText:
I found the .NET 11 breaking changes page. Now let me fetch the specific breaking change pages that are relevant to the user's scenarios:

1. GZipStream empty output → `deflatestream-gzipstream-empty-payload.md`
2. TAR archive invalid checksums → `tar-checksum-validation.md`
3. ZipArchive.CreateAsync lazy loading → `ziparchive-createasync-eager-load.md`
4. MemoryStream maximum capacity → `memorystream-max-capacity.md`

Let me fetch all four in parallel.

data.content:
All four scenarios hit documented **.NET 11 breaking changes**. Here's what breaks:

---

### 1. GZipStream "empty = zero bytes" check — **BROKEN**
Previously, compressing zero bytes produced a 0-length output. Now GZipStream **always writes headers/footers** (20 bytes for GZip, 2 for Deflate), even for empty payloads. Your `output.Length == 0` check will never be true.

**Fix:** Check the *uncompressed* data length before compression, or special-case empty input to skip compression entirely.

---

### 2. TAR archives with invalid checksums — **BROKEN**
`TarReader` now **validates header checksums** and throws `InvalidDataException` on mismatch. Your legacy hand-crafted TARs with bad checksums will fail.

**Fix:** Wrap reads in `try/catch (InvalidDataException)`, or fix the legacy tool to emit correct checksums. There is no opt-out flag.

---

### 3. ZipArchive.CreateAsync lazy loading — **CHANGED**
`CreateAsync` now **eagerly loads** all entries (reads the central directory asynchronously upfront). Entries are no longer lazily read when you access `.Entries`. Malformed-entry exceptions now surface at `CreateAsync` time, not at property access.

**Fix:** Move your error handling from `.Entries` access to the `await CreateAsync(...)` call.

---

### 4. MemoryStream max capacity — **REDUCED & DIFFERENT EXCEPTION**
Max capacity dropped from `int.MaxValue` (`0x7FFFFFFF`) to `0x7FFFFFC7` (= `Array.MaxLength`). Exceeding it now throws `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` instead of `OutOfMemoryException`.

**Fix:** Cap allocations at `Array.MaxLength`, and update catch blocks to handle `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` in addition to `OutOfMemoryException`.
